reviews.author: Chuck Lever
reviews.summary:
reviewer noted that the patch does not include a Reviewed-by tag from the original author of the code being modified

Reviewer noted that the patch does not include a Reviewed-by tag, which is required for kernel patches.
reviews.raw_body:
From: Chuck Lever <chuck.lever@oracle.com>

On Sun, 31 May 2026 08:06:57 -0400, Jeff Layton wrote:

---

Applied to nfsd-testing, thanks!

[1/6] nfsd: size fh_verify server sockaddr slot by xpt_locallen
      commit: bbbc993f3932be9428ebf81009d0d1a4aed26d00
[2/6] nfsd: release path refs on follow_down() error
      commit: 41c9073b511fe70d5e3b03f039c409d2060de06b
[3/6] nfsd: fix nfsd_file leak on inter-server COPY setup failure
      commit: b55429cfce784ffcbaed2960d66b7c5db90ceec3
[4/6] nfsd: fix dentry ref leak on V4ROOT export filehandle lookup
      commit: 657c7e3082589c35695f77821f1dfa31bf4c6a34
[6/6] nfsd: fix layout fence worker double-reference race
      commit: 4faa69d7158a4436d340b7324aa34fa672362b5c

--
Chuck Lever <chuck.lever@oracle.com>

reviews.author: Chuck Lever
reviews.summary: Reviewer noted that on the v4.0 replay path, op_release is not called unconditionally after every operation, leading to a memory leak of two posix_acl refs, and requested that op->u be released on this branch as well.

reviews.author: Jeff Layton (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledged that the patch does not handle v4.0 clients issuing OPEN requests with POSIX ACL attributes, agreed to fix in a future revision.
sentiment_signals: acknowledged a need for a fix
reviews.raw_body:
Good catch, will fix.

I initially had hoped that we could rely on the fact that a v4.0 client
couldn't issue an OPEN with a POSIX ACL attribute in it, but it turns
out that knfsd doesn't gate attributes by minorversion.

Maybe it should? That's a separate changeset though if so.
-- 
Jeff Layton <jlayton@kernel.org>

2026-05-31.patch_summary: This patch series addresses medium-severity bugs in the NFS server code, including memory leaks and refcounting issues that can be triggered remotely. The patches fix problems such as file handle verification, path reference management, and layout fence worker synchronization. The changes aim to prevent potential buffer overruns and improve overall stability of the NFS server.

reviews.author: Al Viro
reviews.summary: Reviewer Al Viro suggested unifying the path_put() calls in nfsd_cross_mnt() by providing an example diff, which would simplify the code and improve maintainability.

reviews.author: Jeff Layton (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledged that the patch is reasonable and has already been taken into another tree, but suggested sending an official patch for cleanup.
reviews.raw_body:
Looks reasonable. Chuck has already taken this patch into his tree, but
we could do a cleanup on top. Want to send us an "official" patch?

Thanks,
-- 
Jeff Layton <jlayton@kernel.org>

reviews.author: Al Viro
reviews.summary: Reviewer Al Viro noted that the patch is completely untested and only compiles, requesting additional testing before approval.

reviews.author: Chuck Lever
reviews.summary: reviewer stated that any of the patches in this series could be replaced by existing patches in the nfsd-testing branch
reviews.raw_body:
We can replace any of the patches already in nfsd-testing, it's a topic
branch.


-- 
Chuck Lever

2026-06-01.patch_summary: This patch series addresses medium-severity bugs in the NFS server code, including memory leaks and refcounting issues that can be triggered remotely. The patches fix problems such as file handle verification, path reference management, and layout fence worker synchronization. The changes aim to prevent potential buffer overruns and improve overall stability of the NFS server.
